event: presented to the emergency department of our hospital
no history of any focal neurological deficit
neutrophilic leukocytosis (total leukocyte count – 24,170/μl with neutrophils – 91%)
Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) was sent for evaluation
Gram stain of CSF: polymorphs and lymphocytes with Gram-positive bacilli
CSF cytology: high cell count (1080/cm) with 60% neutrophils and 40% lymphocytes
blood culture on reevaluation recognized as Listeria monocytogenes
Culture of CSF grew Listeria monocytogenes
Colony on blood agar and chocolate agar: Grayish, translucent, smooth, nonhemolytic, about 1 mm in diameter
Gram stain of isolate: Gram-positive coccobacilli

Biochemical reaction: Catalase positive, oxidase negative, CAMP positive, motile at 25°C, nonmotile at 37°C, glucose and mannitol fermenting, hydrolyzed bile esculin, MR positive, VP positive, indole positive, hydrolyze urea, utilize citrate
Sensitivity by Kirby Bauer’s disc diffusion method: Sensitive-amikacin, clarithromycin, meropenem, netilmicin, piperacillin tazobactam, amoxicillin clavulanic acid, imipenem
Sensitivity by Kirby Bauer’s disc diffusion method: Resistant- ceftriaxone
